matched_requirements: Introduces a standardized HANDOFF section before S0.
Defines mandatory trigger scenarios for cross-stage/session/reviewer transitions.
Provides a markdown template with status, goal, fact-source paths, completed work, decisions, unresolved issues, next-role tasks, recommended Skill/reviewer, and desensitization confirmation.
States that REQ/DESIGN/ReviewReport remain the fact sources and handoff only indexes/navigates.
Requires reference-not-copy and prohibits credentials/token/PII/patient data.
missing_requirements: Not all key breakpoints are explicitly covered: S3 design to baseline review, S5 PASS to S6, and S7 to S8 are missing from the mandatory trigger list.
Template lacks handoff reason/trigger, next-step entry criteria, and explicit blocker/risk fields.
Desensitization is mandatory but lacks an operational check/failure procedure.

findings.description: Mandatory trigger scenarios do not cover all key breakpoints, especially S3 design to baseline reviewer, S5 PASS to S6 release, and S7 acceptance to S8 archive.
findings.evidence: The new trigger list includes only S2→S3, S3 baseline review→S4, S4→S5 code review, S5 conditional pass fix→review, S6→S7, and any cross-session continuation. Existing SOP also has S3 B review, S5 C review PASS to S6, and S7 to S8 transitions.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit triggers or a mandatory catch-all for any role/review-gate transfer, with examples for S3→baseline review, S5 PASS→S6, and S7→S8.

findings.description: Template lacks handoff reason/trigger, next-step entry criteria, and blocker/risk fields.
findings.evidence: The template has status, goal, fact-source paths, completed items, decisions, unresolved issues, next-role tasks, recommended Skill/reviewer, and desensitization confirmation, but no reason/entry-criteria/risk fields.
findings.recommendation: Add sections for trigger scenario, next-step entry criteria, and blockers/risks.

findings.location: SKILL.md@feat/handoff-standard lines 94-103; existing S5 gates lines 239-248
findings.description: Desensitization is stated as mandatory but has no operational check or failure handling, and HANDOFF files are not explicitly included in S5 path/secrets checks.
findings.evidence: The template only has a checkbox and the rule prohibits credentials/token/PII/patient data; S5 checks mention path existence and no credential leakage but not HANDOFF specifically.
findings.recommendation: Require generated handoff files to pass path-existence and secret/PII checks; unchecked desensitization should block handoff.

findings.location: SKILL.md@feat/handoff-standard line 56; existing design archive spec lines 341-351
findings.description: New design/HANDOFF-*.md files are compatible with design/ but are absent from the design archive file list.
findings.evidence: The PR sets path design/HANDOFF-{from}-to-{to}.md, while the archive spec lists DESIGN.md, DISCUSSION-LOG.md, LEARNING-LOOP.md, and SHARE-LOG.jsonl only.
findings.recommendation: Add HANDOFF-*.md to the design archive spec and define naming for repeated reviews/cross-session continuations.

findings.description: SOP behavior changes are added while version remains 1.4.0.
findings.evidence: VERSION and SKILL.md both report v1.4.0 on the feature branch.
findings.recommendation: If this PR is released, bump VERSION/tag during S6 or record the version change in the release path.

most_important_recommendation: Turn HANDOFF from a template-only addition into a gate-backed mechanism: cover all critical handoff points and include path validity plus desensitization checks in S5/S8.
